system_architecture: Hybrid Microkernel + Quantum Simulation Layer + AI Orchestration
description: A Python-first, hybrid classical-quantum, AI-native, cross-device operating system designed to replace existing OSes on all device classes.

feature_tiers.TODAY: Implementable now on classical hardware using simulation and existing libraries
feature_tiers.EXPERIMENTAL: Near-term; requires careful engineering; known instability possible
feature_tiers.FUTURE: Requires hardware (QPU, NPU) not yet mainstream on consumer devices

required_agent_skills: Python 3.12+ asyncio and concurrent programming (asyncio.Lock, create_task, gather)
Python C-extension writing via ctypes and Cython for hardware-critical paths
Quantum circuit simulation with Qiskit, Cirq, and PennyLane
NumPy state-vector quantum simulation (complex arrays, Kronecker products, unitary gates)
Operating system microkernel architecture design (IPC, scheduling, memory management)
Hardware abstraction layer (HAL) engineering in Python with ctypes bindings
On-device machine learning inference with ONNX Runtime and llama-cpp-python
LSTM neural network design for time-series resource prediction
Post-quantum cryptography implementation (CRYSTALS-Kyber, Dilithium via liboqs)
AES-256-GCM and SHA3-512 cryptographic operations with the Python cryptography library
Zero-trust security architecture and capability-based permission systems
Containerisation technologies (Wine, QEMU user-mode, LXC) for app compatibility
Kivy cross-platform GUI framework for adaptive desktop/mobile UI
Local speech-to-text integration (Whisper-tiny, Vosk)
Async networking with aiohttp, DNS-over-HTTPS, mDNS/Zeroconf, and WireGuard
Content-addressable storage and multi-stage data compression (LZMA, delta encoding)
Semantic file search using sentence-transformers and vector similarity
pytest test suite authoring with fixtures, mocks, and async test support
Sphinx documentation generation from Python docstrings
Docker containerisation and GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline configuration
QEMU virtual machine management for OS development and testing
development_rules: NEVER generate impossible hardware claims. Always use 'quantum-inspired', 'simulated', or 'classical approximation' language when true quantum hardware is unavailable.
ALWAYS label every feature with its tier: TODAY / EXPERIMENTAL / FUTURE. Unlabelled features are invalid output.
ALWAYS include try/except error handling, logging module calls, Python type hints, and Google-format docstrings in every generated file.
NEVER generate installer code that proceeds without first displaying the full EULA and requiring the user to type 'I AGREE' exactly.
ALWAYS separate simulation code from future QPU interface hooks using '# TODO: QPU integration' markers.
ALWAYS generate pytest tests for every module as part of the same stage that generates the module.
NEVER claim Python can replace C/ASM for bare-metal boot or context-switch code. Document C stubs as pseudocode; expose them via ctypes bindings.
ALWAYS enforce that AI training data stays on-device by default. Any cloud sync of AI data requires opt-in consent code.
NEVER allow the compatibility layer to claim full iOS/Apple support. Mark iPhone as BLOCKED due to Apple's Secure Enclave.
ALWAYS produce modular code — one class per responsibility, one file per subsystem, following clean architecture principles.
ALWAYS prioritise algorithmic efficiency over brute-force processing. Never claim to magically increase physical hardware speed.
ALWAYS include '# TODO' comments on all interfaces designed for future QPU hardware integration.
ALWAYS ensure HMAC-SHA256 signing is applied to all IPC messages in IPCBus.
NEVER include hard-coded credentials, API keys, or secrets in any generated file.
ALWAYS generate a rollback function alongside every installer action that modifies system state.

stage1: Full folder structure stubs (empty __init__.py in each package)

code_style_requirements.error_handling: try/except on all I/O, hardware, and network operations
code_style_requirements.logging: use Python logging module; DEBUG for internals, INFO for lifecycle events, WARNING/ERROR for failures
code_style_requirements.comments: explain WHY not just WHAT; mark future hardware hooks with # TODO: QPU integration
code_style_requirements.testing: pytest; aim for >80% coverage per module; include async tests with pytest-asyncio
code_style_requirements.architecture: one class per responsibility; one module per subsystem; no circular imports
code_style_requirements.security: never hardcode secrets; sanitise all external inputs; validate hashes before loading any binary
code_style_requirements.naming_convention: snake_case for functions/variables; PascalCase for classes; UPPER_SNAKE for constants

security_constraints.ios_support_status: BLOCKED — Apple Secure Enclave prevents installation

performance_targets.storage_reduction_future: up to 90% (requires ML lossy compression for cold data)
performance_targets.ai_inference_speedup: 5-10x vs CPU-only via ONNX Runtime + GPU
performance_targets.physical_limit_disclaimer: Software cannot exceed physical hardware speed. All gains are algorithmic.
